violations.finding: Bare `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID` invocation without venv activation. The same file correctly uses 'activate venv and run' for other scripts (line 36), but this new Step 0 section omits venv activation.
violations.recommendation: Change to: source venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID

violations.finding: Second bare `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID` invocation without venv activation, same pattern as line 170 in the same new Step 0 section.
violations.recommendation: Change to: source venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID

violations.finding: Step 9 instructs 'The orchestrator invokes `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID`' without venv activation. This is an orchestrator-side invocation instruction, not a subagent prompt template.
violations.recommendation: Change to: 'The orchestrator invokes `source venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID`'

standards_passed: no-inline-code: No new executable inline code blocks introduced in changed files. Code blocks in changelog-analyst.md are pre-existing spec/instructional patterns for agent execution, not new inline scripts.

no-hardcoded-values: aggregate-dev-report.py uses CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var or __file__ resolution for project root. write-commit-grant.py uses CLI args with defaults. No hardcoded absolute paths introduced.
integer-step-numbering: No decimal (N.M) or lettered (Na) step numbering found in changed files.
meaningful-naming: No forbidden naming patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, *-v2, improved-*, better-*, new-*) found in new scripts or references.
english-only: No Chinese characters found in commands/*.md or agents/*.md (unconditional scan performed).
merge-similar-scripts: aggregate-dev-report.py serves a distinct purpose (canonical aggregate writer for parallel-dev cycles) not duplicated by existing scripts.
concise-documentation: New Step 6b in commit.md is appropriately concise with clear status table. changelog-analyst.md structured output section is concise. No marketing language found.
parameterized-scripts: aggregate-dev-report.py accepts --task-id, --dry-run via argparse. write-commit-grant.py now also accepts --revoke-existing-for-task. Both scripts fully parameterized.
dangling-references: scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py exists on disk. commands/close.md and commands/dev.md reference it correctly.
merge-conflicts: No unresolved merge conflict markers found in any changed file.

advisory_findings.finding: Line reads '**Example aggregate JSON** (written by `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID`)' — this is an example label describing what produced the example output, not an operational invocation instruction. Standard 3 exempts lines in example context.
advisory_findings.disposition: not_flagged_as_violation — example/label context; the standard exempts 'Example:' lines and equivalents

advisory_findings.finding: Line contains 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/spec-check.py mark' inside a SECOND ACTION block template that the orchestrator pastes into subagent dispatch prompts. This is a pre-existing pattern outside this cycle's diff scope.
advisory_findings.disposition: out_of_scope — line not changed by this cycle; --changed-files mode excludes pre-existing violations outside the diff

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed all three critical violations (close.md:170, close.md:177, dev.md:709) as genuine Standard 3 breaches. Codex agreed that dev.md:747 is example-label context and should not be flagged as a blocking violation. Codex noted dev.md:171 contains a pre-existing python3 pattern in subagent-template context outside the changed-files diff scope. No false positives identified in the draft violations. No additional critical violations surfaced.

findings.issue: Violations 1-3 (close.md:170, close.md:177, dev.md:709) correctly identified as Standard 3 breaches
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed these are genuine bare python3 invocations without venv activation in operational (non-example) instruction context

findings.issue: Violation 4 (dev.md:747) is example-label context, not an operational invocation
findings.proposed_fix: PROPOSED_FIX: Do not flag dev.md:747 as a blocking violation; treat as advisory-only noting the example-label exemption applies
findings.rationale: The line '**Example aggregate JSON** (written by ...)' is a documentation label, not an instruction to invoke python3. Standard 3 exempts example context. Moved to advisory_findings.

findings.issue: dev.md:171 (python3 in SECOND ACTION subagent template) is outside the changed-files diff scope
findings.proposed_fix: PROPOSED_FIX: Exclude from this cycle's violations report; document as out-of-scope pre-existing finding
findings.rationale: Line was not changed by this cycle. Per --changed-files mode, pre-existing violations outside the diff are out of scope. Documented in advisory_findings.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Advisory disposition applied to dev.md:747 (example-label exemption) and dev.md:171 (out-of-scope pre-existing). Three critical violations retained unchanged. Summary counts reflect actual violations array length (3 critical).
